title: (No AI) You can now ask Google Maps ‘complex, real-world questions’ — and Gemini will answer
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![An image of the Google Maps logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/03/STK270_GOOGLE_MAPS_D.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google is continuing to [weave](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/31/24283970/google-maps-gemini-ai-answer-questions) [Gemini](https://www.theverge.com/news/813588/google-maps-gemini-ai-chatbot-navigation-directions) [into](https://www.theverge.com/2023/10/26/23932315/google-maps-ai-immersive-view-ev-charging-search) [the](https://www.theverge.com/tech/870019/gemini-will-be-your-tour-guide-while-using-googles-walking-directions) [firmament](https://www.theverge.com/2024/2/1/24057994/google-maps-generative-ai-llm-local-guide-search) [of its most-used products](https://www.theverge.com/news/822240/google-ai-mode-travel-plans-canvas). Today, it announced that Google Maps was getting a new AI-powered "Ask Maps" feature that allows for "complex, real-world questions" with highly detailed, personalized responses.

In the past, Google Maps would struggle with hyper-specific questions like "where can I charge my phone without having to wait in line for coffee" or "where is the closest public bathroom that isn't completely disgusting" (parents of kids with tiny bladders, I'm looking at you). But now, thanks to Gemini, all our weird, slightly embarrassing questions can be answered - directions inc …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/893262/google-maps-gemini-ai-ask-maps-immersive-navigation)

Google is continuing to weave Gemini into the firmament of its most-used products. Today, it announced that Google Maps was getting a new AI-powered "Ask Maps" feature that allows for "complex, real-world questions" with highly detailed, personalized responses.
In the past, Google Maps would struggle with hyper-specific questions like "where can I charge my phone without having to wait in line for coffee" or "where is the closest public bathroom that isn't completely disgusting" (parents of kids with tiny bladders, I'm looking at you). But now, thanks to Gemini, all our weird, slightly embarrassing questions can be answered - directions inc …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Perplexity’s Personal Computer turns your spare Mac into an AI agent
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/2lFbEKLJQ88PaaoZZTWuGV5rQY.avif?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Perplexity wants to be more than just an answer engine. On Wednesday, it launched Personal Computer, a new AI agent tool that can turn a spare Mac into a locally run AI system, pitching it as "a digital proxy for you."

[Personal Computer](https://www.perplexity.ai/hub/blog/everything-is-computer) will run 24/7 on a dedicated device on your local network, have full access to your files and apps, and be controllable from anywhere and on any device, Perplexity said. That deeper access makes it a more personalized version of a similar product Perplexity [launched last month](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/884778/perplexity-computer-combines-ai-chatbots-and-agents-into-one-system), Perplexity Computer, a cluster of agents it described as a "general-purpose digital worker."

Perplexity pitches the system as more …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/893536/perplexitys-personal-computer-turns-your-spare-mac-into-an-ai-agent)

Personal Computer will run 24/7 on a dedicated device on your local network, have full access to your files and apps, and be controllable from anywhere and on any device, Perplexity said. That deeper access makes it a more personalized version of a similar product Perplexity launched last month, Perplexity Computer, a cluster of agents it described as a "general-purpose digital worker." 
Perplexity pitches the system as more …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Miles Klee
title: (No AI) Grammarly Is Facing a Class Action Lawsuit Over Its AI ‘Expert Review’ Feature
dc:creator: Miles Klee
content: The feature, which Grammarly shut down Wednesday, presented editing suggestions as if they came from established authors and academics—without their consent.
contentSnippet: The feature, which Grammarly shut down Wednesday, presented editing suggestions as if they came from established authors and academics—without their consent.
